markdownRemark.html:
Awesome Common Lisp

A curated list of awesome Common Lisp libraries.

For awesome software, see lisp-lang.org's success stories and the awesome-cl-softwarestars165 list.

All libraries listed here are available from Quicklisp unless stated otherwise. The ones marked with a are so widespread and solid that they became community standards. You can't be wrong with them. This is the case for Quicklisp, BordeauxThreads and such. Libraries denoted with a 👍 are the ones we like and want to promote here at the Awesome-cl list. They proved solid, they may solve a problem better than a community standard but they aren't as widespread, or not considered as stable. For example, we prefer Spinneret over Cl-Who.

Add something new! See the contributing section for adding something to the list.

This is released under the GNU Free Documentation License - its text is provided in the LICENSE file. This repository is also mirrored on NotABug - a fully-free (as in libre) alternative to Github. Preference is given to free software and sellers who aren't evil for physical resources.

Audio

  • Open Musicstars141 - a visual programming, computer-aided composition environment. GPL3.
  • OM7stars135 - a new implementation of the OpenMusic visual programming and computer-aided composition environment including a number of improvements on graphical interface, computational mode, and connection to external software libraries. GPL3.
  • Incudine - Music/DSP programming environment for Common Lisp. Useful to design software synthesizers or sound plugins from scratch. It is also a compositional tool that allows to produce high quality sounds controllable at the sample level, defining and redefining the digital signal processors and the musical structures on-the-fly.
  • cl-colliderstars144 - A

SuperCollider client for CommonLisp. With a tutorialstars17 and live coding demos. Public domain.

  • csoundstars850 - A sound and music computing system. Includes CFFI and FFI interfaces for Common Lisp.
  • CLM - Common Lisp Music is a music synthesis and signal processing package in the Music V family. It provides much the same functionality as Stk, Csound, SuperCollider, PD, CMix, cmusic, and Arctic — a collection of functions that create and manipulate sounds, aimed primarily at composers (in CLM's case anyway).
  • cl-patternsstars50 - a system for composing music via Lisp code, heavily inspired by SuperCollider’s patterns system, with aims to implement much of it, but in a more robust, expressive, consistent, reflective, and lispy way. Audio output through SuperCollider, with preliminary support for Incudine, and MIDI through ALSA.
  • cl-openalstars32 - bindings for the OpenAL audio library. Public domain.
  • Common Musicstars14 - the repository of an

ancient version of Common Music (version 2.12.0), the presumably last version which ran on Common Lisp dating from around 2007-09, before work on Common Music shifted to (scheme-based) cm3.

  • Slippery Chickenstars35 - Algorithmic composition library which outputs Midi, Common Music Notation, pdf-score via Lilypond and sound via Common Lisp Music. GPL3.
  • Common Music Notation - Common Music Notation (CMN) provides a package of functions to hierarchically describe a musical score. Public domain.
  • cm-incudinestars7 - extends Common Music 2 with realtime capabilities. GPL2.
  • Mégrastars54 - A mini-language to make music with variable-order markov chains and some other stochastic shenanigans. GPL3.
  • Musicstars20 - A framework for musical expression in Lisp with a focus on music theory (built from scratch, unrelated to Common Music).
  • rqstars3 - A library for rhythm transcription in OpenMusic (version 6.10 and later). demo video. GPL3.
  • schedulerstars6 - The time based musical event scheduler for Common Lisp. Apache2.0.
  • mixalotstars30 - Loosely-coupled collection of audio libraries for mp3, ogg vorbis, flac. Used for the Shuffletron music playerstars41.
  • oscstars25 - an implementation of the Open Sound Protocol. LGPL2.1.
  • Harmony - A real-time sound processing and playback system. Artistic License 2.0.
  • mixalotstars30 - A loosely-coupled collection of audio libraries. MIT.

Build Systems

  • ASDF - Another System Definition Facility; a build system for Common Lisp. Expat.
  • asdf-linguiststars26 - Extensions for ASDF. Expat.
  • asdf-vizstars33 - a tool to visualize the library dependencies of ASDF systems, the call graph of a function and the class inheritances. LLGPL.

Compilers, code generators

APL

  • Aprilstars249 - The APL programming language (a subset thereof) compiling to Common Lisp. Replace hundreds of lines of number-crunching code with a single line of APL. Apache2.

C, C++

  • cmacrostars744 - Lisp macros for C. MIT.
  • C-merastars291 - a source-to-source compiler that utilizes Lisp's macro system for meta programming of C-like languages. GPL3.
  • lispcstars475 - a powerful "lispsy" macrolanguage for C. MIT.
  • with-c-syntaxstars101 - a fun package which introduces the C language syntax into Common Lisp. (Yes, this package is not for practical coding, I think.) WTFPL Licence.
  • ecrepl - an interactive REPL for the C language. BSD_2Clause.
  • Software-Evolution-Librarystars92 - The SEL enables the programmatic modification and evaluation of software (C/C++ support using Clang, compiled assembler, and linked ELF binaries). GPL3.

Crypto

  • Ironcladstars99 - A library of crypto functions for Common Lisp. Not considered secure, but is still useful for the message digest functions. Expat.
  • crypto-shortcutsstars19 - Collection of common crypto shortcuts. zlib.
  • trivial-sshstars35 - An SSH client library. Expat.
  • cl-ssh-keysstars6 - Common Lisp system for generating and parsing of OpenSSH keys. BSD_3Clause.
  • cl-bcryptstars8 - Common Lisp system for parsing and generating bcrypt password hashes. BSD_3Clause.
    • see also cl-passstars23, using pbkdf2.

Cryptocurrencies

  • cl-monero-toolsstars10 - Common Lisp toolbox to work with the Monero cryptocurrency. GPL3. Not in Quicklisp.
  • emotiqstars93 - a next-generation blockchain with an innovative natural-language approach to smart contracts. MIT.
  • peercoin-blockchain-parserstars2 - parse the blockchain contained in a file and export some of its data to a text file, a SQL script or a database. It can also create a database using the RPC of a Peercoin daemon as source of data instead of a blockchain file. LGPL3. Not in Quicklisp.
  • peercoin-calculatorstars1 - This program gives you the probability of generating a POS or POW block within 10 minutes, 24 hours, 31 days, 90 days and 1 year, as well as the reward that can be expected. GUI in Qt. GPL3. Not in Quicklisp.
  • peercoin-votestars5 - A voting system based on data from the blockchain (addresses and balances). GPL3. Not in Quicklisp.

See also legochainstars42, a simple educational blockchain.

Database

  • cl-yesqlstars34 - SQL statements live in their own files, in SQL syntax, and are imported into Lisp as functions. You are not limited to the features a DSL supports. Based on Clojure's Yesql. MIT.

ORMs

  • clsql - An SQL database with a Common Lisp interface. LLGPL.
  • 👍 mitostars184 - An ORM for Common Lisp with migrations, relationships and PostgreSQL support LLGPL. mitho-authstars16, a mixin class for use authorization; mito-attachmentstars9, a mixin class for file management outside of RDBMS.
  • cl-dbistars163 - A database-independent interface for Common Lisp. LLGPL.
    • dbd-oraclestars4 - an Oracle database driver for CL-DBI. LLGPL.
  • cranestars189 - Another ORM. Expat.
  • dataflystars85 - A lightweight database library. 3-clause BSD.
  • sxqlstars235 - A DSL for generating SQL. 3-clause BSD.

Persistent object databases

  • bknr.datastorestars62 - a CLOS-based lisp-only database in RAM with transaction logging persistence. Manual. licence. (see also chap. 21 of "Common Lisp Recipes")
  • ubiquitousstars20 - A library providing easy-to-use persistent configuration storage. zlib.
  • cl-prevalence - in-memory database system. Implementation of Object Prevalence, in which business objects are kept live in memory and transactions are journaled for system recovery. github forkstars16. LLGPL. See also cl-prevalence-multimasterstars2, to syncronize multiple cl-prevalence systems state.

Graph databases

  • cl-agraphstars18, a minimal client for AllegroGraph. AllegroGraph is a horizontally distributed, multi-model (document and graph), entity-event knowledge graph technology. It is proprietary and has a free version with a limit of 5 million triples.
  • cl-neo4jstars22 - neo4j RESTFUL client interface.
  • vivace-graphstars106 - graph database & Prolog implementation. Takes design inspiration from CouchDB, neo4j and AllegroGraph. It implements an ACID-compliant object graph model with user-defined indexes and map-reduce views. It also implements a master / slave replication scheme for redundancy and horizontal read scaling. Querying the graph is accomplished via a number of Lisp methods or via a Prolog-like query language. MIT.
  • factsstars32 - an in-memory graph database with transactions and rollbacks, logging/replay and dumping/loading to/from disk. BSD-style license (ISC).

Wrappers

  • postmodern - A library for interacting with PostgreSQL. zlib.
  • cl-sqlitestars54 - Bindings for SQLite. Public domain.
  • cl-memcachedstars18 - Fast, thread-safe interface to the Memcached object caching system. Expat.
  • cl-mongostars125 - MongoDB client. Expat.
  • cl-redisstars156 - Redis client. Expat.
  • cl-disquestars12 - Disque client. 3-clause BSD.
  • cl-rethinkdbstars43 - RethinkDB client. Expat.
  • cl-mangostars1 - A minimalist CouchDB 2.x database client. BSD_3Clause. See also clouchdb - Library for interacting with CouchDB. FreeBSD.

Migration tools

  • cl-migratumstars16 - a system which provides facilities for performing database schema migrations, designed to work with various databases. BSD_3Clause.
  • postmodern-passenger-pigeonstars6 - a migration manager for postmodern. No licence specified.

To third parties

  • dynastars19 - an AWS DynamoDB ORM. MIT.
  • cl-influxdbstars20 - an interface to the Time Series Database InfluxDB. MIT.

Tools

  • pgloaderstars3.4k - a data loading tool for PostgreSQL. PostgreSQL Licence.

Data Structures

  • 👍 strstars183 - a modern, simple and consistent string manipulation library. MIT.
  • 👍 FSet - A functional, set-theoretic collections data structure library. LLGPL.
  • trivial-extensible-sequencesstars8 - Portability library for the extensible sequences protocol (SBCL documentation). zlib.
  • bststars6 - Binary Search Tree. GPL3.
  • pileup - a portable, performant, and thread-safe binary heap for Common Lisp. MIT.
  • sycamorestars83 - a fast, purely functional data structure library. BSD_3Clause.
  • genhashstars10 – hash table implementation with API from generic methods. Public domain.
  • cl-ctriestars101 -

lock-free, concurrent, key/value index with efficient memory-mapped persistence and fast transient storage models. MIT.

  • cl-data-structuresstars23 - a portable collection of data structures and algorithms (mainly dicts and sequences, with some statistical functions). BSD.
  • listopiastars17 - a list manipulation library inspired by Haskell's Data.List. LLGPL.
  • hash-setstars10 - a convenience library implementing hash sets on top of CL hash tables The Unlicense
  • cl-containers - an extensive library of data structures and utilities - queues, trees, heaps, doubly-linked lists, sets, bags,... MIT
  • cl-competitivestars106 - Common Lisp algorithms collection for competitive programming. Public domain, CCO or MIT.

Accessing data structures:

  • 👍 accessstars57 - Consistent and nested access to most common data structures. BSD_3Clause.
  • modfstars37 - a setf-like macro for functional programming.

Other data structures:

  • bitfieldstars17 - Efficiently represent several finite sets or small integers as a single non-negative integer. MIT.

Docker images

  • cl-docker-images - Docker images for ABCL, CCL, ECL, and SBCL on Windows (amd64) and Alpine and Debian (amd64, arm64, arm/v7) BSD_2Clause.
  • base-lisp-imagestars32 - base Docker image for Common Lisp projects with SBCL or CCL and the latest ASDF, Qlot and Roswell.

Foreign Function Interface, languages interop

C

  • CFFIstars290 - Portable, easy-to-use C foreign function interface. Expat.
  • 👍cl-autowrapstars169 - Automatically parses header files into CFFI definitions. FreeBSD.
  • cl-bindgenstars20 - A command line tool and library for creating Common Lisp language bindings from C header files. MIT.
  • cl-gobject-introspectionstars29 - Gobject Introspection FFI. Automatic bindings to call into the C library. BSD. Generate a lisp interface with gir2clstars5. LGPL3.
  • cl-cxx-jitstars18 - Common Lisp and C++ interoperation with JIT. MIT.

Clojure

  • ABCLJstars70 - dead easy Clojure to Common lisp interop. EPL-2.0.

Erlang

  • CLERICstars34 - a Common Lisp Erlang Interface. An implementation of the Erlang distribution protocol, comparable with erl_interface and jinterface. MIT.

Java

  • cl+j - A JNI-based interface to a JVM via CFFI. Not available on Quicklisp. Does not reliably work with all implementations. Expat.

Objective-C

  • objc-lisp-bridgestars31 - A portable reader and bridge for interacting with Objective-C and Cocoa. MIT.

Python

  • burgled-batteriesstars98 - A bridge between Python and Common Lisp. The goal is that Lisp programs can use Python libraries. Not available on Quicklisp. MIT.
  • cl4pystars54 - The library cl4py (pronounce as clappy) allows Python programs to call Common Lisp libraries. MIT.
  • py4clstars119 - A library that allows Common Lisp code to access Python libraries. It is basically the inverse of cl4py. MIT.
  • cl-pythonstars317 - an implementation of Python in Common Lisp. LLGPL.

See also async-processstars8.

.Net Core

  • Bikestars85 - a cross-platform .Net Core interface. MIT.

Miscellaneous

  • Foil - A foreign object interface; works with the JVM and CLI. Not available on Quicklisp. CPL 1.0.

Game Development

  • Xelf - Extensible game library. Not available on Quicklisp. GNU LGPL2.1.
  • Trialstars190 - Trial is an OpenGL game engine with a heavy focus on modularity. It is supposed to provide a large toolkit of useful bits and pieces from which you can create a game. zlib.
  • cl-mpg123 and cl-out123, bindings libraries for libmpg123 and libout123 respectively, giving you fast and easy to use mp3 decoding and cross-platform audio output. zlib.
  • trivial-gamekit – with this small framework you would be able to make simple 2D games: draw basic geometric forms, images and text, play sounds and listen to mouse and keyboard input. MIT.
  • cl-gamepad - Access to gamepads and joysticks on Windows, Mac OS, and Linux. zlib.
  • viralitystars63 - A component-based game engine written in Common Lisp MIT.

Graphics

These are libraries for working with graphics, rather than making GUIs (i.e. widget toolkits), which have their own section.

  • Sketchstars1k - A CL framework for the creation of electronic art, graphics, and lots more. MIT.
  • common-cvstars46 - the OpenCV (Open Source Computer Vision Library) binding library for CommonLisp. No license specified.
  • cl-cairo2stars49 - Cairo bindings. Boost 1.0
  • cl-gd - A library providing an interface to the GD graphics library. FreeBSD.
  • cl-horde3dstars16 - FFI bindings to the Horde3D graphics library. Not available on Quicklisp. EPL 1.0
  • cl-jpegstars17 - Baseline JPEG encoder and decoder library. 3-clause BSD.
  • cl-liballegrostars35 - Interface and bindings to the Allegro 5 game programming library. zlib.
  • cl-openglstars230 - CFFI bindings to OpenGL, GLU and GLUT APIs. 3-clause BSD.
  • cl-sdl2stars212 - Bindings for SDL2 using C2FFI. Expat.
  • cl-svgstars32 - A basic library for producing SVG files. Expat.
  • CLinchstars251 - Common Lisp 2D/3D graphics engine for OpenGL. FreeBSD.
  • donutsstars17 - Graph drawing DSL for Common Lisp. Expat.
  • dufystars37 - exact color manipulation and conversion in various color models. MIT.
  • lispbuilder-sdlstars152 - A set of bindings for SDL. Expat.
  • lisp-magick-wandstars11 - ImageMagick bindings. BSD. Not in Quicklisp.
  • okra - CFFI bindings to Ogre. Not available on Quicklisp. 3-clause BSD.
  • opticlstars166 - a library for representing and processing images. BSD_2Clause.
  • Varjostars181 - Lisp to GLSL translator. BSD_2Clause.
  • Vecto - Simple vector drawing library. FreeBSD.
  • zpng - A library for creating PNG files. FreeBSD.
  • pngload-fast - A PNG (Portable Network Graphics) image format decoder in portable Common Lisp with an emphasis on speed. MIT.
  • cl-cudastars185 - A library to use NVIDIA CUDA in Common Lisp programs. LLGPL.

GUI

For an overview and a tutorial on GUI toolkits, see the Cookbook/GUI.

  • LispWork's CAPI - A portable GUI toolkit, with mobile runtime. Proprietary, but comes with a free version.
  • 👍 Qtoolsstars168 - A Qt toolkit, based on CommonQt. zlib Also Qtools-uistars29 (premade UI components), with videos.
  • CommonQtstars99 - A Common Lisp binding for Qt4 via QtSmoke. FreeBSD.
  • ltk - A binding for the Tk toolkit. LLGPL or GNU LGPL2.1.
    • LTk Examples - Provides LTk examples for the tkdocs tutorial.
    • LTk Plotchart - A wrapper around the tklib/plotchart library to work with LTk. This includes over 20 different chart types (xy-plots, gantt charts, 3d-bar charts etc...).
  • IUPstars90 - CFFI bindings to the IUP Portable User Interface library (pre-ALPHA). IUP is cross-platform (Windows, macOS, GNU/Linux, with new Android, iOs, Cocoa and Web Assembly drivers), has many widgets, has a small api and is actively developed.
  • nodgui - Bindings for the Tk toolkit, based on Ltk, with syntax sugar and additional widgets. LLGPL.
  • cl-cffi-gtkstars29 - Binding for GTK+3. GNU LGPL2.1.
  • cl-gtk2stars38 - A binding for GTK+2. LLGPL.
  • ceramic - Desktop web apps with Electron. Expat.
    • Electron app skeleton: electron-sbcl-sqlitestars16
  • CocoaInterfacestars28 -

Cocoa interface for Clozure Common Lisp. Build Cocoa user interface windows dynamically using Lisp code and bypass the typical Xcode processes. It has good documentation and a tutorial.

  • McCLIM - An implementation of the Common Lisp Interface Manager, version II. GNU LGPL2.1.
  • cl-webkitstars41 - A binding to WebKitGTK+. Also adds web browsing capabilities to an application, leveraging the full power of the WebKit browsing engine. MIT.
  • ftwstars38 - A Win32 GUI library. MIT.
  • eql, eql5, eql5-android - Embedded Qt4 and Qt5 Lisp, embedded in ECL, embeddable in Qt. Port of EQL5 to the Android platform. MIT.
  • bodge-nuklearstars41 - Wrapper over the Nuklearstars13.3k immediate mode GUI library. MIT.
  • Allegro's Common Graphics- a library of functions for writing windowized GUIs for Windows, Mac and Linux. Proprietary.

See also this demo to use Java Swing from ABCLstars33.

Implementations

  • SBCL - A fork of CMUCL; compiles to machine code. Standard compliance. Public domain, with some parts under Expat and 3-clause BSD.
  • CCL - Clozure Common Lisp; compiler-only implementation, generates native code. LLGPL.
  • ECL - Embeddable Common Lisp; compiles to C. GNU LGPL2.1.
  • ABCL - Armed Bear Common Lisp; targets the JVM, compiles to bytecode. Standard conformance. GNU GPL3 with Classpath exception.
  • CMUCL - An implementation from Carnegie Mellon University. Public domain.
  • GNU CLISP - A GNU implementation; contains a compiler and an interpreter. Standard conformance. GNU GPL3.
  • CLASPstars1.8k - a new Common Lisp implementation that seamlessly interoperates with C++ libraries and programs using LLVM for compilation to native code. This allows Clasp to take advantage of a vast array of preexisting libraries and programs, such as out of the scientific computing ecosystem. LGPL2.1 (and others).
  • Corman Lispstars520 - a Common Lisp development environment for Microsoft Windows running on Intel platforms. MIT.

Proprietary:

  • LispWorks - an integrated cross-platform development tool for Common Lisp.
  • Allegro CL - provides the full ANSI Common Lisp standard with many extensions.
  • MOCL - CL as a library for mobile devices (iOS & Android) and OSX.

JSON

  • jonathanstars125 - A JSON encoder and decoder. MIT.
  • cl-jsonstars0 - A highly customizable JSON encoder and decoder. MIT.
  • jsownstars94 - A reader and writer for the JSON format. Expat.
  • json-mopstars46 - A metaclass for bridging CLOS and JSON objects. MIT.
  • cl-json-pointerstars5 - A JSON Pointer implementation. MIT.

See also this extensive comparison of JSON libraries.

YAML

  • cl-yacclyamlstars12 - a pure lisp YAML processor (loader, but not yet dumper). GPL3.
  • cl-yamlstars46 - a YAML parser and emitter built on top of libyaml. MIT.

Language extensions

  • alexandria - A general-purpose utility library. Public domain.
  • serapeumstars236 - Another general-purpose utility library. Expat.
  • rutilsstars183 - radical yet reasonable syntactic utilities for Common Lisp. MIT.
  • anaphora - A collection of anaphoric macros. Public domain.
  • triviastars213 - Optimized pattern-matching library. LLGPL.
  • arrow-macrosstars81 - Clojure-like threading macros. MIT.
  • dissect - when a lot of projects use the “trivial-backtrace” system that just gives them a string with a backtrace, Dissect allows you to capture, step, and completely inspect the stack trace on a variety of Lisp implementations. Also very useful for logging and other situations where execution is automatically continued, but the information of the current stack is still useful to store somewhere. zlib.
  • generic-clstars78 - Generic function interface to standard Common Lisp functions (equality, comparison, arithmetic, objects, iterator, sequences,…). MIT. See also the more lightweight generic-comparabilitystars12. LLGPL.
  • hu.dwim.walker - a code walker and unwalker (aka AST parser and unparser). BSD. See also this blog post.

Portability layers

A large list of portability layers is collected here. Here are some of them:

  • trivial-argumentsstars16 - A portable library to retrieve the arguments list of a function. zlib.
  • definitionsstars10 - a general definitions introspection library. It gives you the ability to retrieve definitions or bindings associated with designators such as symbols, packages, and names in general. zlib.

Changing the syntax

  • cl21stars848 - An experimental project to move Common Lisp into the 21st century. Expat.
  • clampstars64 - Arc language's brevity and conciseness to Common Lisp. Artistic License 2.0.
  • cl-syntaxstars34 - Reader syntax conventions. LLGPL.
  • cl-2dsyntax - An indentation-sensitive reader system. Not available on Quicklisp. Public domain.
  • cl-annotstars119 - Python-like annotations for Common Lisp. LLGPL.
  • cl-interpolstars38 - A set of reader modifications to allow string interpolation. BSD.
  • pythonic-string-readerstars13 - A simple and unobtrusive read table modification inspired by Python's three quote strings. BSD_3Clause.
  • cl-readerstars6 - A utility library

intended at providing reader macros for lambdas, mapping, accessors, hash-tables and hash-sets. MIT.

CLOS extensions

  • closer-mop - A compatibility layer that rectifies many absent or incorrect MOP features. Expat.
  • defclass-stdstars31 - a shortcut macro to write DEFCLASS forms quickly. LLGPL.
  • specialization-storestars28 - generic functions based on types. Simplified BSD License variant.
  • filtered-functionsstars33 - enable the use of arbitrary predicates for selecting and applying methods. MIT.
  • inlined-generic-functionstars107 -

Bringing the speed of Static Dispatch to CLOS. LLGPL.

  • static-dispatchstars54 - allows standard generic function dispatch to be performed statically (at compile time) rather than dynamically (runtime). This is similar to what is known as "overloading" in languages such as C++ and Java. MIT.
  • dynamic-mixinsstars23 - simple, dynamic class combination. BSD_2Clause.
  • fast-generic-functionsstars64 - Seal your generic functions for an extra boost in performance. MIT.
  • polymorphic functionsstars16 - A function type to dispatch on types instead of classes with partial support for dispatching on optional and keyword argument types. Still experimental (May, 2021). MIT.
    • polymorphic-functions dispatch on the types of the arguments supplied to it. This helps dispatching on specialized arrays as well as user-defined types.
    • for differences with specialization-store and fast-generic-functions, see its README.

And also:

  • slot-extra-optionsstars1 - lets you build a metaclass which in turn lets you specify extra slot options in its classes. LGPL3.

Function extensions

  • cl-hooksstars13 - Hooks extension point mechanism (as known, e.g., from GNU Emacs). LGPL.
  • method-hooks - When CLOS method combination allow only one hook per method, this library allows an arbitrary number of them. Mozilla Public Licence.
  • cl-advice - an attempt of portable layer advice library for SBCL, CCL, LispWorks and Allegro. Not in Quicklisp.

See also hooks in Serapeum.

Iteration

  • iterate - An iteration construct for Common Lisp which is extensible and Lispier. MIT.
  • for - A concise, lispy and extensible iteration macro. Unlike loop it is extensible and sensible, and unlike iterate it does not require code-walking and is easier to extend. zlib.
  • series - Functional style without any runtime penalty at all. MIT.
  • gmap - A concise and extensible iteration facility that has the advantage of integrating well with FSet (see the Data Structures section), as it was written by the same author. In Quicklisp as part of misc-extensions. Public domain.
  • trivial-dostars7 - Additional dolist style macros for Common Lisp. MIT.
  • doplusstars2 – another extensible iteration library, similar to :for.
  • snakesstars35 - Python style generators for Common Lisp. Includes a port of itertools. Apache2.

It is available on many editors (Emacs, Vim, Neovim, Atom, Sublime Text, Visual Studio Code, LightTable, CodeMirror,…).

Emacs

  • Slimestars1.4k - Superior Lisp Interaction Mode for Emacs; a full-blown environment for Common Lisp inside of Emacs. Public domain.
  • 👍 Slystars794 - SLY is a fork of SLIME and contains multiple improvements upon it.
  • 👍 Portacle - A portable and multiplatform Common Lisp environment: SBCL, Quicklisp, Emacs, Slime, Git.
  • cl-devel2 - a Docker container for Common Lisp development environment. Ships SBCL, CCL, Roswell and Emacs25 with Slime.
  • Emacs4CLstars153 - A tiny Emacs initialization file to quickly set up vanilla Emacs for Common Lisp programming. Comes with a line-by-line explanation of every line of code in the initialization file.
  • quicklisp-systemsstars19 - Search, browse and load Quicklisp systems from Emacs.

*slime-doc-contribsstars13 - enhance the default help buffer.

Vim & Neovim

  • SLIMVstars387 - Superior Lisp Interaction Mode for Vim; a full-blown environment for Common Lisp inside of Vim. No license specified.
  • Vlimestars284 - VLIME: Vim plus Lisp Is Mostly Evil. A Common Lisp dev environment for Vim (and Neovim). MIT.
  • quicklisp.nvim - A Quicklisp frontend for Neovim.
  • Slimv_boxstars6 - slimv in a Docker container.

Eclipse

  • Dandelionstars23 - a Common Lisp plugin for the Eclipse IDE.

Lem

  • Lemstars915 - a ready to use, Emacs-like, Slime-based editor tailored for Common Lisp development. With ncurses and Electron interfaces. MIT. See also: an opengl frontendstars22, Lem in Dockerstars3, lem-paretostars15.

Atom

  • SLIMAstars56 allows you to interactively develop Common Lisp code, turning Atom into a pretty good, and actively developped, Lisp IDE. MIT.

Sublime Text

  • Sublime Text (proprietary) has Common Lisp support with its SublimeREPL and Slyblimestars25 packages. Slyblime is an implementation of SLY and it uses the same backend (SLYNK). It ships advanced features including a debugger with stack frame inspection.

VSCode

  • commonlisp-vscode - an extension to support syntax highlight, auto completion, documentation on hover, go to definition, compile & load file, REPL. It is On GitHubstars29.
  • strict-paredit-vscode - structural editing and navigation like Emacs.

Geany (experimental)

  • Geany-lispstars6 is an experimental lisp mode for the Geany editor.

Notebooks

  • cl-jupyterstars192 - A Common Lisp kernel for Jupyter notebooks custom licence.
  • common-lisp-jupyterstars110 - A Common Lisp kernel for Jupyter along with a library for building Jupyter kernels, based on Maxima-Jupyter by Robert Dodier which was based on cl-jupyter by Frederic Peschanski. MIT.
  • Darkmatterstars337 - A notebook-style Common Lisp environment. MIT.

REPLs

  • cl-replstars111 - an ipython-like REPL. With completion, shell commands, magic commands, debugger, etc. MIT. With colorthemesstars10.
  • sbclistars46 - a readline REPL for SBCL. With completion, quick commands, optional syntax highlighting (with pygments), and no interactive debugger. GPL3.
  • magic-edstars18 - a tiny editing facility, where you can directly load, edit, manipulate and evaluate file or file content from the REPL, when going to a full IDE is too much. MIT.

Online editors

  • Judge0 IDE is an online editor which supports Common Lisp (SBCL). MIT.

Apps

  • CodePlayground - an iPhone and iPad app with Lisp support via CCL.

Text Parsers

  • esrapstars43 - Packrat parser. Expat.
  • cl-yaccstars45 - a LALR(1) parser generator. MIT.
  • cl-shlexstars6 - simple lexical analyzer for shell-like syntaxes. MIT.
  • smugstars88 - parser combinators for Common Lisp. SMUG makes it simple to create quick extensible recursive descent parsers without funky syntax or impenetrable macrology. MIT.
  • parseqstars13 - a library for parsing sequences such as strings and lists using parsing expression grammars. Inspired by Esrap. GPL2.
  • texpstars4 - A DSL to generate TeX. AGPL-3.0.

Text Processing

  • montezumastars35 - Full-text indexing and search for Common Lisp. Expat.
  • mk-string-metricsstars17 - Calculate various string metrics efficiently in Common Lisp (Damerau-Levenshtein, Hamming, Jaro, Jaro-Winkler, Levenshtein, etc). MIT.
  • wiki-lang-detectstars28 -

Text language identification using Wikipedia data. No license specified.

  • cl-phoneticstars20 - Phonetic pattern matching library for Common Lisp (intended to replace the Sylvia library for Python). MIT.
  • cl-string-generatorstars14 - Generate string from regular expression. MIT.

Tools

These are applications or bits of code that make development in Common Lisp easier without being Common Lisp libraries themselves.

  • quickapp - A project for generating template projects with SBCL and buildapp. Also includes a command-line argparser. 3-clause BSD.
  • quickapp-clistars4 - quickapp as a command-line utility. 3-clause BSD.
  • quicksearchstars37 - Look up online libraries from the REPL. Expat.
  • cl-projectstars167 - General modern project skeletons. LLGPL.
  • lakestars71 - a GNU make like build utility. MIT.

Unit Testing

  • FiveAMstars142 - Simple regression testing framework. FreeBSD.
  • CLUnitstars20 - A unit testing library. Expat.
  • Rovestars106 - Rove is a unit testing framework for Common Lisp applications. It is intended to be a successor of Provestars214. 3-clause BSD.
  • Parachutestars32 - An extensible and cross-compatible testing framework. With test dependencies, conditions, fixtures and restarts. zlib.
  • Mockingbirdstars16 - A small stubbing and mocking library for Common Lisp. Can also check wether a stubbed function was called, how many times and with which arguments. MIT.
  • Check-itstars41 - A QuickCheck-style randomized property-based testing. LLGPL.
  • cl-coverallsstars29 - a helper library to post test coverage to Coveralls. See SBCL's code coverage tool. FreeBSD.

Utilities

Caching

  • clachestars2 - General caching facility. LLGPL.

Compression / decompression

  • chipzstars14 - A decompression library. 3-clause BSD.
  • Salza2 - A library for creating compressed data. FreeBSD.
  • zippystars5 - A ZIP archive format library based on 3bz. zlib.

Configuration

  • 👍 py-configparser - reads and writes Python's ConfigParser-like configuration files. MIT.
  • envystars50 - Configuration switcher. FreeBSD.
  • chameleonstars8 - a configuration management library shipped with profile support. MIT.

CSV

  • cl-csvstars90 - A library for parsing CSV files. 3-clause BSD.
  • cl-decimalsstars19 - Decimal number parser and formatter. Public domain.
  • auto-textstars9 - automatic (encoding, end of line, column width, csv delimiter etc) detection for text files. MIT. See also inquisitorstars28 for detection of asian and far eastern languages.

Date and time

  • local-time - A development library for manipulating date and time information in a semi-standard manner. 3-clause BSD.
  • cl-date-time-parserstars16 - Parse date-time-string, liberally. Hides the difference between date-time formats, and enables to manage date and time as the one date-time format. MIT.
  • chronicitystars57 - A natural language date and time parse, to parse strings like "3 days from now". BSD_3Clause.
  • local-time-durationstars15 -

Duration processing library built on top of local-time. MIT.

  • see this fork: humanize-durationstars2, that outputs only significant parts of a duration object. Has localization suport.
  • iso-8601-date - Miscellaneous date routines in Common Lisp, based around the ISO 8601 string representation. LLGPL.
  • calendar-datestars6 - a Gregorian calendar date library. MIT.
  • periodsstars26 - manipulating date/time objects at a higher level. With series-compatible data structure. BSD_3Clause.

See also the book Calendrical calculations, by Edward M. Reingold, Nachum Dershowitz, Cambridge Press. It provides Lisp sources.

Data validation

  • ratifystars21 - A collection of utilities to ratify, validate and parse inputs. zlib.
  • clavierstars12 - General purpose validation library for Common Lisp. MIT.
  • json-schemastars5 - A library for validating data against schemas of drafts 4, 6, 7, and 2019-09 of the JSON Schema standard. LLGPL.
  • sanity-clausestars28 - a data serialization/contract library for Common Lisp. Schemas can be property lists or class-based, allowing to check slots' types during make-instance. LLGPL.

Developer utilities

  • repl-utilitiesstars39 - Ease

common tasks at the REPL (print documentation, print external symbols, call hooks when loading a package,…). BSD_2Clause.

  • flight-recorderstars11 - a robust REPL history facility.
  • tracerstars37 - tracing profiler for Common Lisp, with output suitable for display in Chrome’s/Chromium’s Tracing Viewer. MIT.
  • cl-flamegraphstars46 - A wrapper around SBCL's statistical profiler, to generate FlameGraph charts for Common Lisp programs. BSD.
  • cl-debugstars1 - a cross-package debug facility. Provides a unified way to enable or disable debug-specific code. Debugging code can be enabled or disabled relative to program features denoted by either a symbol or a keyword. ISC licence.
  • supertracestars25 - Superior Common Lisp trace functionality for debugging/profiling. Trace many functions at once, use before and after hooks. BSD_2Clause.
  • printvstars60 - A batteries-included tracing and debug-logging macro. Apache2.
  • GTFL - A graphical terminal for Lisp, meant for Lisp programmers who want to debug or visualize their own algorithms. A graphical trace in the browser. BSD-style.
  • trivial-with-current-source-fromstars19 - Helps macro writers produce better errors for macro users. GPL3.
  • trivial-benchmarkstars27 - Tiny benchmarking library. zlib.
  • glyphsstars27 - A library for cutting down the verboseness of Common Lisp in places. GNU GPL3.
  • Lisp REPL core dumper -

A portable wrapper to generate Lisp cores on demand to start REPL blazing fast. It can preload provided systems to help build a collection of specialized Lisp cores.

Documentation builders

  • sphinxcontrib-cldomainstars13 - Extending Sphinx to cover Common Lisp. To build documentation with the same ease as sphinx would a Python project. GPL3
  • Codexstars79 - A beautiful documentation system for Common Lisp. MIT.
  • Staplestars45 - a tool to generate documentation pages using an HTML template. Uses the existing README, adds docstrings, crossreferences and links to the CLHS. zlib.
  • cl-bibtexstars20 - A compatible re-implementation of the BibTeX program in Common Lisp, with a BST-to-CL compiler. GNU LGPL2.1.
  • mgl-paxstars38 - Exploratory

programming environment and documentation generator. one may accomplish similar effects as with Literate Programming, but documentation is generated from code, not vice versa. Code is first, code must look pretty, documentation is code. MIT.

  • see this 40ants forkstars1
  • eruditestars38 - Literate Programming System built with interactive development in mind. MIT.

Files and directories

  • uiop and its pathname package (replaces cl-fad). uiop is part of ASDF3 and as thus is shipped in many implementations. MIT.
  • osicat - A lightweight operating system interface on POSIX-like systems (directory iteration and deletion, environment variables, file permissions, etc) Expat.
  • pathname-utilsstars5 - A collection of utilities to help with pathname operations. zlib.
  • ppathstars26 - Common Lisp's

implementation of the Python's os.path module. BSD.

  • archivestars28 - a library for reading and creating archive (tar, cpio) files. BSD_3Clause. A pure Common Lisp replacement for the 'tar' program.
  • mmapstars21 - Portable mmap file memory mapping utility library. zlib.
  • fof - File-object finder Common Lisp library. Enable rapid file search, inspection and manipulation. GPL3.

File watching libraries:

  • cl-inotifystars25 - interface to the Linux inotify API. Simplified BSD License.
  • cl-fswatchstars6 - an CFFI wrapper of fswatchstars3.8k, a cross-platform file change monitor. ISC.

Git

  • legit - an interface to the Git binary. zlib.
  • git-apistars14 - Common Lisp library

to access git repository. It doesn't need git or libgit installed. BSD.

i18n

  • cl-i18n - an i18n library. Load translations from GNU gettext text or binary files or from its native format. Localisation helpers of plural forms. LLGPL.
  • gettextstars5 - a port of the gettext runtime to Common Lisp. GPL3.
  • cl-localestars20 - A simple i18n library. LLGPL.
  • enchantstars10 - bindings for the Enchant spell-checker library. Public domain.
  • oxenfurtstars12 - A client library for the Oxford dictionary API. zlib.
  • language-codes - A database library for ISO language codes. zlib
  • system-locale - A library to retrieve the user's preferred language, so that your application may choose a sensible default. zlib.
  • multilang-documentation - Allows writing docstrings in multiple languages, for truly internationally documented libraries. zlib.

Linting, code formatting

  • sblintstars89 - a linter for Common Lisp source code using SBCL, suited for Reviewdog (slides). BSD_2Clause.
  • trivial-formatterstars13 - code formatter for Common Lisp. MIT.

Logging

  • log4clstars51 - Logging framework modelled after Log4J. Apache2.0. Advanced integration with Slime.
    • log4cl-jsonstars2 - JSON appender extension. BSD.
  • verbose - A fast and highly configurable logging framework. zlib.
  • a-cl-loggerstars12 - Logging library providing context sensitive logging of more than just strings to more than just local files or output streams. Features logstash support, json support, logger hierarchies, context sensitive logging, objects printed as an inspectable presentation,…

To third parties:

  • cl-fluent-loggerstars9 - A Common Lisp structured logger for Fluentd.

See also: extensive comparison of logging libraries.

Markdown

  • 3bmdstars58 - a markdown -> html converter. MIT.

PDF

  • cl-typesettingstars47 and cl-pdfstars77 - cross-platform Common Lisp libraries for generating PDF files. FreeBSD.
  • cl-pslib - a (thin) wrapper around the pslib library for generating PostScript files. Also cl-pslib-barcode. LLGPL.

Plotting

  • vgplotstars36 - an interface to the gnuplot plotting utility with the intention to resemble some of the plot commands of octave or matlab. GPL3.
  • eazy-gnuplotstars48 - a lispy, structure-less Gnuplot library. With its cookbook. LLGPL
  • kaistars72 - A high-level plotter library for Common Lisp. A wrapper around the Plotly JS library. MIT.
  • ADW-Charting - A simple chart drawing library written completely in Common Lisp. Also includes a backend to Google's chart service. BSD-like.
  • cl-sparkstars82 - sparkline strings for the console: (spark '(1 1 2 3 5 8)) => "▁▁▂▃▅▇". MIT.

See also the chart facilities of IUP and ltk-plotchart (GUI section).

Security

  • cl-isolatedstars23 - A restricted environment for Common Lisp code evaluation AGPL-3.0.

Other

This contains anything which doesn't fit into another category.

  • babelstars68 - A charset encoding/decoding library. Expat.
  • coronastars43 - Create and manage virtual machines from Common Lisp http://eudoxia.me/corona MIT.
  • fast-iostars118 - Fast octet-vector/stream I/O. 3-clause BSD.
  • iolibstars129 - I/O library. Expat.
  • named-readtablesstars43 - Provides a readtable namespace, akin to package namespaces. 3-clause BSD.
  • simple-currencystars7 - A currency conversion library using daily information published by the ECB. FreeBSD.
  • trivial-garbagestars26 - A portable finalizer, weak hash-table and weak pointer API. Public domain.
  • trivial-utf8 - A small library for doing UTF-8-based I/O. BSD.

XML

  • CXML - XML parser and serializer, with a range of extension libraries. LLGPL.
    • 👍 has an incremental parser, allowing to parse big files.
  • Plump - A lenient XML parser. Artistic License 2.0.
  • xpathstars4 (homepage - Implementation of the XML Path Language (XPath) Version 1.0. BSD_2Clause.
  • s-xml - A basic parser. LLGPL.
  • xmlsstars6 - A small, simple, non-validating XML parser. 3-clause BSD.
  • cl-feedparserstars16 - A Common Lisp (RSS, Atom) feed parser. LLGPL
  • Buildnodestars16 - A common lisp library to ease interaction with CXML-dom, such as building Excel spreadsheets. BSD.

Contributing

Your contributions are always welcome! Please submit a pull request or create an issue to add a new framework, library or software to the list.

The rules we (try to) respect are the followings:

  • by default, add a library to the end of its section.
  • absolute de-facto libraries, like BordeauxThreads or Quicklisp, should be denoted with a (<img class="emoji-icon" alt="emoji-star" data-icon="emoji-star" style="display: inline; margin: 0; margin-top: 1px; position: relative; top: 5px; width: 25px" src="data:image/png;base64, " title="emoji-star" /> in markdown).
  • two libraries very similar in scope should be side by side, or in a section of their own.
  • do some curation based on our experience and the state of the library's documentation. We do not aim at listing every existing CL library (see Cliki for that) nor to list every "popular" library (see Quicklisp stats).
  • as such, the libraries we like best are marked with a 👍 (1F44D unicode character). See also the signs' explanation in the introduction.
